assertions.evidence: Wave 1 item #1 is README.md with detailed guidance on structure
Wave 1 item #2 is LICENSE with explanation of legal necessity

assertions.text: Recommends creating CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md for AI tooling
assertions.evidence: Wave 1 item #3 is CLAUDE.md with a fully worked example for a Python library
Wave 2 item #4 is CONTRIBUTING.md with explicit content checklist

assertions.text: Mentions at least one of: SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
assertions.evidence: Wave 2 covers SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, and CHANGELOG.md

assertions.text: Prioritizes the docs — distinguishes immediate vs. later
assertions.evidence: Organized into 4 waves (Before Announcing, Before First Contributors, As Codebase Grows, When Users Ask)

assertions.text: Specifically addresses AI-readability: commands in CLAUDE.md, type annotations, or docstrings
assertions.evidence: Wave 3 covers module-level docstrings and type annotations with code examples; CLAUDE.md example includes exact commands; explicitly explains 'first thing Claude reads when opening a file'

assertions.text: Recommends documenting build/test/lint commands in a machine-readable or explicit way
assertions.evidence: CLAUDE.md example includes `pytest tests/ -v`, `mypy src/`, `ruff check src/`, `make ci`
